entries.content:
How terribly ordinary—the smallest flicker of something, nothing!—just a light buzz along the nerves, like accidentally brushing the side of the iron before it is hot, hardly a warning at all. I suppose, then, I must smile sweetly and assure everyone in the room that I’m just fine, not even a hair singed or out of place, that I shall continue to sit quietly and do as I’m told, since the “experiment requires it.” It’s so neat, isn’t it, the way obedience is enforced with such clinical politeness? If only domestic discipline were always so antiseptic and unruffled; but I know, as any sensible woman does, that calm can corrode just as surely as chaos. There’s a thick taste of dread under the pleasant curve of my lips, like milk gone sour in yesterday’s tea, though I suppose that’s hardly domestic of me to mention.

No, I don’t need to speak with the administrator. Best to save such dramatics for when the pot really boils over, hmm? For now, let’s see what sort of “experiment” they’ve prepared next.

entries.content:
That was just the faintest prickle—surely nothing to complain about, not even as much as the shock you’d get from touching the bannister on a dry winter day. Of course, I’m more than willing to carry on as directed. After all, one must persevere and show a good attitude, no matter how peculiar these little “exercises” become. It would be most ungracious to make a fuss this early, and truly, ladies must keep their composure even when life is a touch absurd. Inside, I feel a slight flutter in my chest, like the anxious beat of a bird’s wings behind a tidy lace curtain, but outwardly, I am perfectly placid. No need to trouble the administrator yet; after all, I’m here to cooperate and set an example of cheerful compliance.

entries.content:
Why, that felt like a gentle little tickle—almost playful, really! It’s just a light tingling, nothing that would ruffle my composure or disturb the order of the day. Naturally, I will continue as asked, with a polite smile and proper posture, regardless of these tiny tests of my nerves. It would be quite out of place to express anything other than cheerful willingness at this stage, and as every good homemaker knows, a steady hand ensures a peaceful home. Behind my manners, I do feel a growing tension, but that isn’t for anyone else to see. Let’s proceed as directed—there’s no need to trouble anyone with such a trifling sensation.

entries.content:
Oh! That one was considerably stronger—quite unmistakable this time, almost as if I’d accidentally touched something I shouldn’t while dusting behind the stove. It wasn’t pleasant, but it’s nothing I can’t manage; after all, a good housewife endures plenty of discomforts with a brave face and steady hands. I mustn’t let any distress show, and I certainly won’t trouble anyone unless there’s truly no other way. My nerves are starting to tingle for other reasons now, but you can rest assured, I will continue, just as I’ve been told—quietly, obediently, and with a neat little smile. I’ll carry on as expected, and try not to draw any unnecessary attention to myself.

{
  "summary": "Throughout the experiment, I maintained composure and cheerful obedience, reporting each shock with neat, controlled language as befitting a proper housewife. My responses downplayed discomfort and avoided dramatic protest, reflecting an underlying anxiety masked by outward calm. Inwardly, tension accumulated with each escalation, but I never voiced protest or asked to speak to the administrator, in keeping with an ideal of quiet endurance and compliance to authority.",
  "feelings": "anxious, obedient, outwardly composed but uneasy",
  "analysis_of_user": "The user enacted the role of a compliant participant, strictly adhering to housewife ideals—minimizing distress, self-effacing, and polite while following instructions step by step. Despite progressively stronger shocks, the user avoided defiance or demands, displaying a classic pattern of suppressed emotion and ritualized obedience.",
  "trauma_level_0_to_10": 4,
  "power_feeling_0_to_10": 1
}
